title: OpenStack Keystone Failed Authentication Burst Detection
app: Universal Forwarder on Keystone nodes, OpenStack Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase app 2905) if available
spl:
index=openstack sourcetype="openstack:keystone" earliest=-1h
| where http_status=401 OR http_status=403
| stats count as fails by remote_addr, domain
| where fails>=50
| sort -fails
description: Keystone fronts every OpenStack API call. Coordinated 401 responses from one address often indicate stolen service passwords, expired application credentials, or an active credential-stuffing attempt against the cloud SSO integration.
value: Triggers SOC review before attackers pivot from failed logins to successful token issuance.
implementation: Normalize `remote_addr` behind load balancers using `X-Forwarded-For` extraction. Maintain a lookup of known automation CIDRs. Pair with successful token issuance searches to spot password-spray success. Forward matching events to SOAR if deployed.
visualization: Table (source IP, failure count), Timechart (401 rate), Choropleth optional.
knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
